REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE ONGOING FAILURE TO REASONABLY ACCOMMODATE THE REQUEST FOR IN-UNIT WASHER AND DRYER HOOK UP; THAT SUCH ONGOING FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE HAS A REMEDY AT LAW (AS OPPOSED TO MONEY DAMAGES FOR PAST FAILURES); AND THAT THERE IS NO RISK OF IRREPARABLE HARM FOR SUCH ONGOING FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE. FURTHERMORE, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE REASONABLENESS OF DEFENDANT'S ATTEMPT TO ACCOMMODATE WHICH RESULTED IN PLAINTIFF USING HER SINK TO WASH HER CLOTHES SINCE 2015 TO PRESENT, AND PRESUMABLY IN THE FUTURE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, COURT REPORTER:CAROL HARABURDA CSR #8052. TELEPHONE:(415) 551-4000. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Dwana S. Bain, Esq., (510) 593-8679, appearing for Tracey thompson, Plaintiff
Mary A Watson Fisher, Esq., (714) 634-2522 ext. 366, appearing for San Francisco Housing
Authority, Defendant
Vanessa Pouladian, Esq., (818) 853-5142, appearing for John Stewart Co. and HV, Defendants

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Ms. Fisher stated that she contested the tentative ruling, provided oral argument in support of her position and argued that this complaint involves past acts that do not pertain. Ms. Bain argued that this argument is on going and that she is entitle to injunctive relief. Ms. Pouladian submitted on her pleadings.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and show that there are no triable issues of fact as to the ongoing failure to reasonably accommodate the request for in-unit washer and dryer hook up; that such ongoing failure to accommodate has a remedy at law (as opposed to money damages for past failures); and that there is no risk of irreparable harm for such ongoing failure to accommodate. Furthermore, there is a triable issue of fact regarding the reasonableness of defendant's attempt to accommodate which resulted in Plaintiff using her sink to wash her clothes since 2015 to present, and presumably in the future. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Court reporter:Carol Haraburda CSR #8052. Telephone:(415) 551-4000. (D501)
